documentation: Get the widths to appply to these p_subFields

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.dict.Dict.getHeaderStyle
documentation: Return CSS properties for the p_i(th) header cell

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.dict.Dict.getStorableValue
documentation: Gets p_value in a form that can be stored in the database

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.dict.Dict.getComparableValue
documentation: Return a (deep) copy of field value on p_o

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.dict.Dict.getEntryName
documentation: Add suffix "-d-" to the entry name

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.dict.Dict.remove
documentation: Remove entry corresponding to p_key on the value stored on p_o

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.dict.Dict.store
documentation:
Stores p_value on p_o. If some entry from p_value already exists in
the DB value, it is updated, not overwritten, unless p_overwrite is
True.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.dict.Dict.subValidate
documentation: Validates inner fields

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.dict.Dict.getDiffSubValue
documentation:
Produce a diff between this p_old sub-value and its p_new version.
p_texts contain precomputed translated texts about the user that
performed the change.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.dict.Dict.getDiffValue
documentation:
Return a XHTML representation of dict value p_new incorporating the
differences with this p_old version.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.dict.Dict.getHistoryValue
documentation:
For a Dict field, instead of showing the previous p_value in p_o's
history, show a XHTML diff between p_value, as found at index p_i,
and a newer version of it in p_o's history before p_i, or, if not
found, the value as currently stored on p_o.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.dict.Dict.iterValues
documentation: Returns an iterator over p_self's values on p_o

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.dict.Dict.getTotalsInfo
documentation:
Returns info allowing to manage totals while rendering p_self's value
on some object.

A Dict value has the form ~{s_key: Object}~. Keys are fixed and given by
a method specified in parameter "keys". Values are Object instances,
whose attributes are determined by parameter "fields" that, similarly to
the List field, determines sub-data for every entry in the dict. This
field is build on top of the List field.

modules.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_
documentation: Meta-class for a Appy class

classes.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.__init__
documentation: Model-construction-time methods
The following set of methods allow to build the model when making an app
or when starting the server.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getBaseClass
documentation: Returns the name of the class being p_self's base class

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.injectProperties
documentation:
Injects, on p_self.concrete, a property for every field defined on
this class, in order to get and set its value.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getFieldClasses
documentation:
Returns, on p_class_ and its base classes, the list of classes where
fields may be defined.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.readPage
documentation:
While browsing a field, a p_page has been encountered. Add it to
self.phases if not already done.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.checkFieldName
documentation: Ensure this field p_name is acceptable

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.cloneField
documentation: Create a clone from the base field named p_name (see Cloner doc)

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.readFields
documentation:
Create attributes "fields" and "switchFields", as ordered dicts
storing all fields defined on this class and parent classes.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.setField
documentation:
Possibly called by p_self.python's m_update method, this method adds,
to p_self.fields, a new p_field named p_name, or replaces the field
currently named p_name if the name is already in use.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.openHistories
documentation:
Make histories on p_self's objects viewable by anyone having the
"read" permission on these objects.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.readSearches
documentation:
Create attribute "searches" as an ordered dict storing all static
searches defined on this class.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getCreators
documentation: Gets the roles allowed to create instances of this class

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.new
documentation: Create and return an instance of the concrete class
Instance-creation method

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.generateId
documentation:
A method named "generateId" may exist on p_self.python, allowing the
app's developer to choose itself an ID for the p_o(bject) under
creation. This method accepts p_o as unique arg and must return the
ID as a string, or None.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getListTitle
documentation: Gets p_o's title as it must appear within lists of objects
Run-time methods
The following set of methods allow to compute, at run-time,
class-dependent elements.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getPageLayouts
documentation:
Gets the page layouts being applicable to p_o, being an instance of
p_self.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.produceUniqueLinks
documentation:
When producing links ("a" tags) to instances of this class, must
these links always be unique ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.showTransitions
documentation:
When displaying p_self's instance p_o, must we show, on p_layout, the
buttons/icons for triggering transitions ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getCssFor
documentation:
Gets the name of the CSS class to use for styling some p_zone on
instance p_o. If this class does not define a dict or method named
"styles", a defaut CSS class may be retrieved from
p_self.defaultCssClasses.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getSubCss
documentation:
Gets the CSS class to apply to the "sub" layout (=object actions on
a list of objects), in the p_context of a field or search, under or
besides p_o's title. p_index is o's p_index within the current list
of objects.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getTextFor
documentation: Gets the translated text corresponding to this p_zone

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getDeleteConfirm
documentation: Return the JS code that executes when someone wants to delete p_o

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getIconsOnly
documentation:
For base actions (edit, delete, etc), must icons only appear, or
complete buttons containing an icon + a label ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getCreateVia
documentation:
How to create instances of this class ? The answer can be: via a
web form ("form"), via a form pre-filled from a template (a Search
instance) or programmatically only (None).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getCreateLink
documentation:
When instances of p_self must be created from a template (from the
UI), this method returns the link allowing to search such templates
from a popup.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getCreateExclude
documentation:
When an instance of this class must be created from a template
object, what fields must not be copied from the template ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getConfirmPopupWidth
documentation:
Gets the width (in pixels, as an integer value), of the confirmation
popup possibly shown when creating or editing p_self's instances.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.maySearch
documentation: May the user search among instances of this class ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.searchesAreEnabled
documentation:
Although m_maySearch may be True, p_self's searches are not available
from public pages, when the site is configured in not-discreet-login
mode.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getSearchAdvanced
documentation: Gets the "advanced" search defined on this class if present

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.maySearchAdvanced
documentation: Is advanced search enabled for this class ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.isIndexable
documentation:
Is p_self "indexable" ? In other words: do we need to have a Catalog
storing index values for instances of this class ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getCatalog
documentation: Returns the catalog tied to p_self, if it exists

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getIndex
documentation:
Returns the index having this p_name, from p_self's catalog, if it
exists.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getListPods
documentation:
Finds, among p_self's fields, those being Pod fields showable on
search results.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getFields
documentation:
Returns, among p_self.fields, those matching p_expr: a Python
expression receiving variable "field" in its context.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getFieldSets
documentation:
Beyond p_self.fields, p_self.python may define additional, dynamic
fields.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getDynamicSearches
documentation: Gets the dynamic searches potentially defined on this class

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getSearch
documentation: Returns static or dynamic search whose name is p_name

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getGroupedSearches
documentation: Returns self's available searches, grouped

methods.name: py3.appy.model.meta.class_.Class.getField
documentation:

classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.string.NumberSelect

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.string.NumberSelect.__init__
documentation: A typical use of such a validator is for checking field values
representing a selection of pages to print for some document. For example,
if one wants to print pages 2, 5, 7 to 19 and 56, a string field may hold
value "2;5;7-19;56", and validating that such a value is valid may be
performed by this NumberSelect class.
This class also offers methods for determining if a number is among a
range of selected numbers.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.string.NumberSelect.validNumber
documentation: Return True if p_value corresponds to a valid number

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.string.NumberSelect.validRange

documentation: Validates that p_value conforms to p_self

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.string.NumberSelect.getValues
documentation:
Returns value parts from this validated string p_value, as a basis
for determining if a given number is among a range of selected
numbers as validated by this NumberSelect object (p_self).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.string.NumberSelect.isAmong
documentation: Is this integer p_number among p_numbers or p_ranges ?

Validator used for validating a selection of numbers encoded as a
string.

classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.string.String

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.string.String._MODULO_97
documentation:
p_value must be a string representing a number, like a bank account.
this function checks that the 2 last digits are the result of
computing the modulo 97 of the previous digits. Any non-digit
character is ignored. If p_complement is True, it does compute the
complement of modulo 97 instead of modulo 97. p_obj is not used;
it will be given by the Appy validation machinery, so it must be
specified as parameter. The function returns True if the check is
successful.
Some predefined functions that may be used as validators

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.string.String.BELGIAN_NISS

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.string.String.EURO_ZIP

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.string.String.BELGIAN_ZIP
documentation: Returns True if p_value contains a valid Begian postal code

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.string.String.IBAN
documentation:
Checks that p_value corresponds to a valid IBAN number. IBAN stands
for International Bank Account Number (ISO 13616). If the number is
valid, the method returns True.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.string.String.BIC
documentation:
Checks that p_value corresponds to a valid BIC number. BIC stands
for Bank Identifier Code (ISO 9362). If the number is valid, the
method returns True.

classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.group.UiGroup

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.group.UiGroup.__init__
documentation:
A UiGroup can group various kinds of elements: fields, searches,
transitions... The type of content that one may find in this group
is given in p_content.
* p_group      is the Group instance corresponding to this UiGroup;
* p_page       is the Page instance where the group is rendered (for
               transitions, it corresponds to a virtual page
               "workflow");
* p_className  is the name of the class that holds the elements to
               group.

classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.phase.Phase

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.phase.Phase.__init__
documentation: This class is used only in order to generate phase- and page-related i18n
labels, and as general information about all phases and pages defined for
a class in the app's model.
It is not used by the Appy developer: if a phase must be defined by him in
an app, he will do it via a phase name specified as a string, in attribute
"phase" of class Page hereabove.
It is not used by Appy at run-time neither: class UiPhases is used
instead. Indeed, at run-time, the set of phases and pages which are
visible by the logged user can be a sub-set of all statically-defined
phases and pages.
A phase is a group of pages within a class

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.computed.Computed.isCompleteValue
documentation: If you build a custom widget with a Computed field, Appy can't tell if the
value in your widget is complete or not. So it returns True by default.
It is up to you, in method obj.validate, to perform a complete validation,
including verifying if there is a value if your field is required.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.computed.Computed.freeze
documentation:
Normally, no field value is stored for a Computed field: the value is
computed on-the-fly by p_self.method. But if you freeze it, a value
is stored: either p_value if not None, or the result of calling
p_self.method else. Once a Computed field value has been frozen,
everytime its value will be requested, the frozen value will be
returned and p_self.method will not be called anymore. Note that the
frozen value can be unfrozen (see method below).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.file.FileInfo.dump
documentation:
Exports this file to disk (outside the db-controlled filesystem).
The tied p_o(bject) is required. If p_filePath is specified, it
is the path name where the file will be dumped; folders mentioned in
it must exist. If not, the file will be dumped in the OS temp folder.
The absolute path name of the dumped file is returned. If an error
occurs, the method returns None. If p_format is specified,
LibreOffice will be called for converting the dumped file to the
desired format.

classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.data.EventData

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.data.EventData.__init__
documentation: In order to store custom data in a calendar event (within its "data"
attribute), define a class inheriting from this one.
Custom data is to be used when it is not possible or desirable to use
standard Appy objects in a calendar. Indeed, if you want to "store" Appy
objects within calendar fields, a lot easier approach is to create the
Appy objects the standard way, and then store an event whose type is or
includes the Appy object's IID. Custom data allows you to directly inject
and manipulate data in calendar events. Consequently:
- the standard Calendar views will not do much for you to graphically
represent the data, or allow to edit it ;
- some standard functions, like merging events of the same day, will
potentially imply losing custom data.
But it is very likely that you will build custom UI controls to
manipulate, visualize or edit such data.

documentation:
Returns a 2-tuple with the 2 parts one may render about p_self: the
standard attributes as defined in the EventData constructor, and the
sub-class custom rendering.

Abstract base class used to define objects that will be stored within
appy.model.field.calendar.Event objects, in attribute "data".

documentation:

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.getEventsBySlot
documentation:
Returns a list of (s_timeslot, event) tuples for every event defined

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.standardizeDateRange
documentation:
p_range can have various formats (see m_walkEvents below). This
method standardizes the date range as a 6-tuple
(startYear, startMonth, startDay, endYear, endMonth, endDay).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.walkEvents
documentation:
Walks, on p_o, the calendar value in chronological order for this
field and calls p_callback for every day containing events. The
callback must accept 3 args: p_o, the current day (as a DateTime
instance) and the list of events at that day (the database-stored
PersistentList instance). If the callback returns True, we stop the
walk.

If p_dateRange is specified, it limits the walk to this range. It
can be:
* an integer, representing a year;
* a tuple of integers (year, month) representing a given month
  (first month is numbered 1);
* a tuple (start, end) of DateTime instances.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.getEventsByType
documentation: Returns all the events of a given p_eventType

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.hasEventsAt
documentation:

p_events.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.getEventName
documentation:
Gets the name of the event corresponding to p_eventType as it must
appear to the user.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.getAllEvents
documentation:

* the list of all event types (from this calendar and p_others);
* a dict of event names, keyed by event types, for all events
  in this calendar and p_others).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.getStartDate
documentation: Get the start date for this calendar if defined

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.getEndDate

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.getDefaultDate

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.mayDelete

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.mayEdit
documentation: May the user edit calendar events ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.deleteEvent
documentation:
Deletes an event. If t_timeslot is "*", it deletes all events at
p_date, be there a single event on the main timeslot or several
events on other timeslots. Else, it only deletes the event at
p_timeslot. If p_handleEventSpan is True, req.deleteNext will be used
to delete successive events, too. Returns the number of deleted
events.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.deleteEvents
documentation:
Calls m_deleteEvent to delete all events between these p_start and
p_end dates (DateTime objects), transmitting all other parameters to
m_deleteEvent. Returns the number of deleted events.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.calendar.Calendar.validate
documentation:
The validation process for a calendar is a bit different from the
standard one, that checks a "complete" request value. Here, we only
check the validity of some insertion of events within the
calendar.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.select.Select.getPossibleValues
documentation:
Returns the list of possible values for this field.
If p_withTranslations is True, instead of returning a list of string
values, the result is a list of tuples (s_value, s_translation).
Moreover, p_withTranslations can hold a given language: in this case,
this language is used instead of the user language. If
p_withBlankValue is True, a blank value is prepended to the list,
excepted if the type is multivalued. Used in combination with
p_withTranslations being True, the i18n label for translating the
blank value is given in p_blankLabel. If p_className is given, p_o is
the tool and we are called from a context where no object is
available (ie, a search form).

representing the action.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.getOnClick
documentation: Gets the JS code to execute when the action button is clicked

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.getTargetObjects
documentation:
In the context of a multi-action, retrieve the list of target objects
onto which the action must be applied.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.execute
documentation: Execute the action on p_o. Returns a tuple (b_success, s_message)

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.getValue
documentation: Call the action and return the result

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.getStoredValue
documentation: There is no stored value for an action

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.isShowable
documentation: Never show actions on an "edit" p_layout

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.isFake
documentation:
Must the shown button be a fake button ? If yes, the return value is
the message to show with the fake button.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.getInputTitle
documentation:
Returns the content of attribute "title" for the "input" field
corresponding to the action in the ui.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.getIconUrl
documentation:
Returns the URL to p_self's (s)icon (depending on p_pre(fix)), to be
used as a background image or not (depending on p_asBG).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.getInputLabel
documentation:
Returns the label to display on the button corresponding to this
action = the content of attribute "value" for the "input" field.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.getConfirmText
documentation: Get the text to display in the confirm popup

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.isRenderableOn
documentation: Action fields can be rendered on "button" but not on "top" layouts

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.perform
documentation: Called when the action is triggered from the UI

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.getMulti
documentation:
In the context of a multi-action, create and return a Multi instance
defining useful info about the action, like the back and check
hooks.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.setTraversable
documentation:
By default, the URL allowing to trigger an action (/perform) is
accessible only to those having permission 'write' on the
corresponding object. This method allows to update this and open the
action to anyone having this p_allow condition.

inner_classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.Layouts

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.action.Action.Layouts.getDefault
documentation: Default layouts for this Action p_field
Action-specific layouts

An action is a Python method that can be triggered by the user on a
given Appy class. An action is rendered as a button or icon.

classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Show

class_variables.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Show.ER_
documentation: A variant, without "result"

class_variables.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Show.E_S
documentation: A variant, with "sub" instead of "result"

class_variables.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Show.BS
documentation: Both in pxButtons and within lists of objects

class_variables.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Show.BR
documentation: Appropriate for some fields like pods
Provides some frequently used values for field's "show" attributes

classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Initiator

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Initiator.__init__
documentation: The concept of initiator can also be used in the context of visualizing
objects. For example, when displaying a tied object via a Ref, its
"initiator" is considered to be the source object and the source Ref.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Initiator.extractField
documentation: Tries to get the initiator field from the request

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Initiator.getUrl
documentation:
Returns the URL for going back to the initiator object, on the page
showing self.field.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Initiator.checkAllowed
documentation: Checks that adding an object via this initiator is allowed

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Initiator.updateParameters
documentation:
Add the relevant parameters to the object edition page, related to
this initiator.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Initiator.goBack
documentation: Once the object has been created, where must we come back ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Initiator.getNavInfo
documentation:
If m_goBack is "view" and navigation-related information must be
shown on the "view" page of the p_new object, this method returns
it.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Initiator.getBackUrl
documentation: Get the URL to go to after having initiated creation of object p_o

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Initiator.manage
documentation:
Once the p_new object has been created, the initiator must surely
perform a given action with it (ie, for a Ref field, the new object
must be linked to the initiator object. This is the purpose of this
method.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Initiator.isComplete
documentation:
We may have thought that an initiator was there, but once parsed, the
"nav" key may reveal that there is no initiator at all. When it is
the case, this method returns False and the initiator is considered
to be inexistent.

From a given field on a given object, it is possible to trigger the
creation of another object. The most obvious example is the creation of a
tied object via a Ref field with add=True. An "initiator" represents the
(object, field) that initiates the object creation.

In order to customize initiator behaviour, every Field sub-class can
propose an Initiator sub-class, by overriding its static attribute
"initiator".

classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Validator

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Validator.validate
documentation:
Return a translated error message if p_value is not valid, True
else.
This class is not to be confused with the homonym class in package
appy.ui.validator, used to validate data coming from an Appy object as
rendered on the web UI.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Validator.evaluateCustom
documentation:
Perform custom p_field validation on this p_value for this p_o(bject)
if a custom validator has been defined on p_field.validator.

Field-specific Validator class, used in the context of attribute
Field.validate (see Field constructor).

classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.doRender
documentation:
Allows to call pxRender from code, to display the content of this
field on p_o in some specific context, for example in a Computed
field.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.validationPx
documentation: Python implementation of validation PX
Displays validation-error-related info about a field

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.init
documentation: Lazy initialisation

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.__repr__
documentation: Short string representation for this field

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.asString
documentation: p_self's alternate short string representation

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.isMultiValued
documentation: Does this type definition allow to define multiple values?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.umlMultiplicities
documentation: Return p_self's multiplicities in UML format

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.isSortable
documentation:
Can fields of this type be used for sorting purposes, when sorting
search results (p_inRefs=False) or when sorting Ref fields
(inRefs=True) ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getFilterField
documentation:
p_self may be the variant of another field: this latter must be used
when it comes to filtering and sorting. This method returns this
potential filter companion, or p_self if none is defined, together
with the PX to use for filtering.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.isShowable
documentation: When displaying p_o on a given p_layout, must we show this field ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.isRenderable
documentation: Returns True if p_self can be rendered on this p_layout

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.isRenderableOn
documentation: Called by m_isRenderable when p_self.renderable is None

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.isInnerable
documentation: May p_self be used as inner field within an outer field ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.isClientVisible
documentation:
Returns True if p_self is visible according to master/slave
relationships.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.inGrid
documentation: Is this field in a group with style "grid" ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.formatMapping
documentation:
Creates a dict of mappings, one entry by label type (label, descr,
help).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getPx
documentation: Returns the PX corresponding to p_c.layout

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.setMandatoriness
documentation:
Updating mandatoriness for a field is not easy, this is why this
method exists.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.setMaster
documentation: Initialises the master and the master value if any

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.setPage
documentation:
Puts p_self into this p_page. If p_page is None, p_self will be
rendered in the default page.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getCss
documentation:
Complete list p_r with the names of CSS files that are required for
displaying widgets of self's type on a given p_layout. p_r is not a
set because order of inclusion of CSS files may be important.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getJs
documentation:
Completes list p_r with the names of Javascript files that are
required for displaying widgets of self's type on a given p_layout.
p_r is not a set because order of inclusion of Javascript files may
be important.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.isInner
documentation: Returns True if p_self is an inner field within a container field

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getValueAt
documentation:
Gets p_self's stored value on this p_o(bject), p_at this moment (a
DateTime object).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getStoredValue
documentation:
Gets the value in its form as stored in the database, or in the
request if p_fromReq is True. It differs from calling
m_getRequestValue because here, in the case of an inner field, the
request value is searched within the outer value build and stored on
the request.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getRequestValue
documentation: Gets a value for this field as carried in the request

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.setRequestValue
documentation:
Sets, in the request, field value on p_o in its "request" form (=the
way the value is carried in the request).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getRequestSuffix
documentation:
In most cases, in the user interface, there is one homonym HTML
element for representing this field. Sometimes, several HTML elements
are used to represent one field (ie, for non-native dates: one field
for the year, one for the month, etc). In this case, every HTML
element's name has the form <field name><suffix>. This method returns
the suffix of the "main" HTML element.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getValue
documentation: Gets, on p_o(bject), the value for this field (p_self)

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getValueIf
documentation:
Special method only called by the framework. For some fields (or
according to some p_disable condition), value retrieval as performed
here must be disabled.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getCopyValue
documentation:
Gets the value of this field on p_o as with m_getValue above. But
if this value is mutable, get a copy of it.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getComparableValue
documentation:
Get the value of this field on p_o, as can be compared with its new
version, coming from the validator.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getFormattedValue
documentation:
p_value is a real p_o(bject) value from a field from this type. This
method returns a pretty, string-formatted version, for displaying
purposes. Needs to be overridden by some child classes. If
p_showChanges is True, the result must also include the changes that
occurred on p_value across the ages. If the formatting implies
translating some elements, p_language will be used if given, the
user language else.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getShownValue
documentation:
Similar to m_getFormattedValue, but in some contexts, only a part of
p_value must be shown. For example, we may need to display only
a language-specific part of a multilingual field (see overridden
method in string.py).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getXmlValue
documentation:
This method allows a developer to customize the value that will be
marshalled into XML. It makes use of attribute "xml".

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.updateHistoryValue
documentation:
p_values is a dict of field values, of the form
                  ~{s_fieldName: fieldValue}~,
containing values being stored on p_o prior to a change. Before
storing this dict in p_o's history, it may be needed to modify, in
this dict, the (key, value) pair corresponding to p_self.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getHistoryValue
documentation:
Returns p_value as must be shown in the UI within p_o's history. If
p_language is specified, p_value is the p_language-specific part of a
multilingual field. p_value is in p_o's history, at entry whose index
is p_i.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getSearchValue
documentation:
Returns the search value (or interval of values) that has been
encoded in a search form (in p_req) for matching values for this
field. This value may already be passed in p_value.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.searchValueIsEmpty
documentation:
In some search form, must the search value or interval of values
specified for this field (p_self) be considered as empty ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getInlineEditableValue
documentation:
Returns p_value as it must appear on a view layout, with code
allowing to inline-edit it when relevant.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getFakeObject
documentation:
Returns a fake object, to mimic an object storing this p_value for
field p_self.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getIndexType
documentation:
Returns the name of the class corresponding to the index used to
store values of this field in a database catalog, or the class itself
if p_class_ is True.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getIndex
documentation:
Gets the Index object corresponding to this field, or None if the
field is not indexed.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getReindexAction
documentation: Render an icon allowing to entirely reindex p_self's index

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getIndexValue
documentation:
Return a version for this field value on p_o being ready for indexing
purposes.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getCatalogValue
documentation:
Returns the value being currently stored in the catalog for this
field on p_o.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.hasSortIndex
documentation:
Some fields have indexes that prevents sorting (ie, list indexes).
Those fields may define a secondary index, specifically for sorting.
This is the case of Ref fields for example.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getSortValue
documentation:
Return the value of p_self on p_o that must be used for sorting.
While the raw p_value may be the value to use in most cases, it is
not always true. For example, a string like "Gaëtan" could have
"gaetan" as sort value.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.valueIsInRequest
documentation:
Is there a value corresponding to this field in the request? p_name
can be different from self.name (ie, if it is a field within another
(List) field). In most cases, checking that this p_name is in the
request is sufficient. But in some cases it may be more complex, ie
for string multilingual fields.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getMasterInRequest
documentation:
Returns, among these p_fields, the first one having a value in the
p_req(uest).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getStorableValue
documentation:
p_value is a valid value initially computed through calling
m_getRequestValue. So, it is a valid representation of the field
value coming from the request. This method computes the value
(potentially converted or manipulated in some other way) as can be
stored in the database.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getFilterValue
documentation:
Convert this p_value, being a raw value from the UI filter, into a
value that can be used as parameter to perform a search.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getValueFilter
documentation:
Convert this p_value, produced by m_getFilterValue, into its form as
manipulated by a UI filter.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getInputValue
documentation:
Gets the value that must be filled in the "input" widget
corresponding to this field.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.isReadonly
documentation:
Returns True if, when this field is rendered on an edit layout as an
input field, it must have attribute "readonly" set.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.setSlave
documentation:
Sets p_slaveField as slave of this field. Normally, master/slave
relationships are defined when a slave field is defined. At this time
you specify parameters "master" and "masterValue" for this field and
that's all. This method is used to add a master/slave relationship
that was not initially foreseen.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getMasterData
documentation:
Gets the master of this field (and masterValue) or, recursively, of
containing groups when relevant.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getMasterTag
documentation:
Generally, for a field, the name of the tag serving as master for
driving slave fields is the name of the field itself. But sometimes
it can be different (see Field sub-classes).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getMasterCss
documentation:
Get the CSS class(es) to set on a tag corresponding to a field having
this p_master.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getTagCss
documentation:
Gets the CSS class(es) that must apply to XHTML tag representing this
field in the ui. p_tagCss may already give some base class(es).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getOnChange
documentation:
When this field is a master, this method computes the call to the
Javascript function that will be called when its value changes (in
order to update slaves).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.isEmptyValue
documentation: Returns True if the p_value must be considered as an empty value

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.isCompleteValue
documentation:
Returns True if the p_value must be considered as "complete". While,
in most cases, a "complete" value simply means a "non empty" value
(see m_isEmptyValue above), in some special cases it is more subtle.
For example, a multilingual string value is not empty as soon as a
value is given for some language but will not be considered as
complete while a value is missing for some language. Another example:
a Date with the "hour" part required will not be considered as empty
if the "day, month, year" part is present but will not be considered
as complete without the "hour, minute" part.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.validateValue
documentation:
This method may be overridden by child classes and will be called at
the right moment by m_validate defined below for triggering
type-specific validation. p_value is never empty.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.securityCheck
documentation:
This method performs some security checks on the p_value that
represents user input.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.validate
documentation:
Checks that p_value, coming from the request (p_o is being created or
edited) and formatted through a call to m_getRequestValue defined
above, is valid according to this type definition. If it is the case,
None is returned. Else, a translated error message is returned.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.store
documentation:
Stores, on p_o, this p_value, that must comply to p_self's type
definition. When p_value is None, (a) if p_delete is False, the
stored value is left untouched; (b) if p_delete is True, the stored
value is deleted.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.storeValueFromAjax
documentation: Called by m_storeValueFromAjax to save p_value on p_o

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.storeFromAjax
documentation:
Stores the new field value from an Ajax request, or do nothing if
the action was canceled.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.callMethod
documentation:
This method is used to call a p_method on p_o. p_method is part of
this type definition (ie a default method, the method of a Computed
field, a method used for showing or not a field...). Normally, those
methods are called without any arg. But one may need, within the
method, to access the related field. This method tries to call
p_method with no arg *or* with the field arg.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getAttribute
documentation:
Gets the value of attribute p_name on p_self, which can be a simple
value or the result of a method call on p_o.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getDisabled
documentation:
For sub-classes that propose attribute "disabled", this method allows
to answer the question: must p_self be disabled on the "edit"
layout ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getGroup
documentation: Gets the group into wich this field is on p_layout

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getGroups
documentation: Returns groups as a list

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.renderLabel
documentation:
Indicates if the existing label (if hasLabel is True) must be
rendered by pxLabel. For example, if field is an action, the
label will be rendered within the button, not by pxLabel.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getSelectSize
documentation:
If this field must be rendered as a HTML "select" field, get the
value of its "size" attribute. p_isSearch is True if the field is
being rendered on a search screen, while p_isMultiple is True if
several values must be selected.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getInputSize
documentation:
Returns the width of the input field, either in the "size" attribute,
(p_sizeAttr is True) or as a CSS "width" attribute (p_sizeAttr is
False).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getSelectStyle
documentation:
If this field must be rendered as a HTML "select" field, get the
value of its "style" attribute. p_isSearch is True if the field is
being rendered on a search screen, while p_isMultiple is True if
several values must be selected.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getWidgetStyle
documentation:
If this field must be rendered as any widget not being a select
widget (managed by m_getSelectStyle hereabove), and if its width
and/or height are specified as strings, restrict the visible part of
the widget to these values, with a scrollbar when appropriate.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getWidthInChars
documentation:
If attribute "width" contains an integer value, it contains the
number of chars shown in this field (a kind of "logical" width). If
it contains a string, we must convert the value expressed in px
(or in another CSS-compliant unit), to a number of chars.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getListHeader
documentation:
When p_self is used as inner-field, within a table-like rendered
container field, this method returns the content of the header row
corresponding to this inner field.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.getValueIfEmpty
documentation: Get the value to display if the field is empty

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.asXhtml
documentation: Renders a HTML table containing field values for this o_o(bject)

class_variables.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.cellLayouts
documentation: Cell-like layouts

class_variables.name: py3.appy.model.fields.Field.indexType
documentation: For most fields, the base Index class suits
Basic abstract class for defining any field

classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.initSubFields
documentation: Initialises sub-fields' attributes

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.lazyInitSubFields
documentation: Lazy-initialise sub-fields, when known

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.init
documentation: List-specific lazy initialisation

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.checkParameters
documentation: Ensure this List is correctly defined

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.getTableCss
documentation: Return the CSS classe(s) to apply to the main table tag

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.getTableName
documentation:
Returns the content of a "data-name" attribute that is defined in the
table tag corresponding to this field.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.getHeaderStyle
documentation: Return CSS properties for the p_i(th) header cell

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.getWidths
documentation: Get the widths to apply to these p_subFields

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.getField
documentation: Gets the field definition whose name is p_name

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.getSubFields
documentation:
Returns the sub-fields, as a list of tuples ~[(s_name, Field)]~,
being showable, among p_self.fields on this p_layout. Fields that
cannot appear in the result are nevertheless present as a tuple
(s_name, None). This way, it keeps a nice layouting of the table.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.getEntryName
documentation:
Gets the name of the request entry corresponding to the value of
this p_sub-field at this p_row.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.getRequestValue
documentation: Concatenates the list from distinct form elements in the request

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.setRequestValue
documentation:
Sets in the request, the field value on p_o in its "request-
carriable" form.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.getStorableRowValue
documentation:
Gets a ready-to-store Object instance representing a single row,
from p_requestValue.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.list.List.getStorableValue

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.pod.ImageFinder.find
documentation:
If p_url is local to the site running this code, this method returns
the absolute path to the file on disk. Else, it returns None, letting
POD perform a HTTP GET request to the "external" web server.

Used by the POD renderer (XHTML conversion) to retrieve paths to local
images instead of performing HTTP GETs to the server running POD.

documentation:
Gets the "base name" of p_template (or self.template[0] if not
given). The base name is the name of the template, without path
and extension. Moreover, if the template is a pointer to another one
(ie Item.odt.something), the base name integrates the specific
extension. In the example, the base name will be "ItemSomething".

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.hour.Hour.intersection

p_rangeB. Each range is of the form

    ((i_startHour, i_startMinutes), (i_endHour, i_endMinutes))

Never call this method with p_recurse=False.
Field allowing to define an hour independently of a complete date

Mixin class injected into any Field whose content can be multilingual

functions.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.setAttribute
documentation:
Sets on p_class_ attribute p_name having some p_value. If this attribute
already exists, an exception is raised.

functions.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.autoref
documentation:
Defines, a posteriori, a Ref from one class the same one, or to another
one but that could not be directly declared because it would have led to
a circular import.

classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Position

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Position.getInsertIndex
documentation:
Gets the index, within tied objects p_refs, where to insert the newly
created object.

When inserting some object among a list of tied objects, this class gives
information about where to insert it.

classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.RefInitiator

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.RefInitiator.checkAllowed
documentation: Checks that adding an object via self.field is allowed

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.RefInitiator.updateParameters
documentation:
Add the relevant parameters to the object edition page, related to
this initiator.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.RefInitiator.goBack
documentation:
After the object has been created, go back to its "view" page or go
back to the initiator.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.RefInitiator.getNavInfo
documentation: Compute the correct nav info at the newly inserted p_new object

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.RefInitiator.manage
documentation:
The p_new object must be linked with the initiator object and the
action must potentially be historized.

When an object is added via a Ref field, this class gives information
about the initiator Ref field and its object.

classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Separator
documentation:
Withins lists of tied objects, one may need to insert one or several
separator(s). If you need this, specify, in attribute Ref.separator, a
method that will return an instance of this class.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.init
documentation: Ref-specific lazy initialisation

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.initBack
documentation: Initialise the back reference

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.initPx
documentation: Create context variables in v_r for layout "view"

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getDefaultSearch
documentation: Returns, among this dict of p_searches, the one being the default

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getSearches
documentation:
Gets the unique Search instance or the dict of Search instances
as defined or computed via attribute "searches". If p_dictOnly is
True, the method returns None if the result is a unique search.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getSearch
documentation: Returns, among, p_self.searches, the one having this p_name

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getCurrentSearchKey
documentation:
In the context of a ref having p_self.searches, returns the key
storing, in the request, the currently selected search.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getCurrentSearch
documentation:
Returns the name of the currently selected search, from this dict of
p_searches.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getListPx
documentation: Get the sub-PX to display inside pxList

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getListCss
documentation:
Get the CSS classes to apply to the main tag for a Ref rendered as a
list.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.checkParameters
documentation: Ensures this Ref is correctly defined

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.isShowable
documentation: Showability for a Ref adds more rules w.r.t base field rules

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.isRenderableOn
documentation:
Only Ref fields with render = "menus" can be rendered on restricted
layouts.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.valueIsSelected
documentation: In PX "Edit", is object whose ID is p_id selected ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getValue
documentation:
Returns the objects linked to p_o through this Ref field.

* If p_start is None, it returns all referred objects;
* if p_start is a number, it returns p_maxPerPage objects (or
  self.maxPerPage if p_maxPerPage is None), starting at p_start.

If p_single is True, it returns the single reference as an object and
not as a list.

If p_batch is True, it returns an instance of appy.model.utils.Batch
instead of a list of references. When single is True, p_batch is
ignored.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getCopyValue
documentation: Return a copy: it can be dangerous to give the real database value

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.setRequestValue
documentation:
The "request form" for a Ref value is a list of string-encoded
iids.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getComparableValue
documentation: Return a copy of field value on p_o

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getFormattedValue
documentation: Return p_value's string representation

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getXmlValue
documentation: The default XML value for a Ref is the list of tied object URLs

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.normalizeSearchValue
documentation:
Converts this individual search p_val(ue) to an int if it is a
string-encoded int.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getSearchValue
documentation:
Values from a search form are tied object's IIDS encoded as strings,
or another attribute as defined in p_self.indexAttribute. If IIDS,
convert them to integers and manage an and/or operator.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.updateHistoryValue
documentation:
Store, in p_values, tied object's iids and titles instead of the
objects themselves.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getHistoryValue
documentation:
Returns p_self's p_value, as stored in p_o's history at index p_i, as
must be shown in the UI.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getValueAt
documentation: Gets p_self's value on this p_o(bject) p_at this date

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getSelect
documentation:
p_self.select can hold a Search instance or a method. In this latter
case, call the method and return its result, that can be a Search
instance or a list of objects.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getPossibleValues
documentation:
This method returns the list of all objects that can be selected
to be linked as references to p_o via p_self.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getViewValues
documentation:
Gets the values as must be shown on px "view". If p_scope is "poss",
it is the list of possible, not-yet-linked, values. Else, it is the
list of linked values. In both cases, we take the sub-set starting at
p_batch.start.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getLinkedObjectsByMenu
documentation:
This method groups p_objects into sub-lists of objects, grouped by
menu (happens when self.render == 'menus').

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getLinkStyle
documentation:
Gets the CSS styles to apply for rendering the "search" button that
opens the popup for linking objects.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getLinkClass
documentation: Return the CSS classes to apply to the "search button" (link)

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getAddButtonType
documentation: Returns the type of the input tag representing the "add" button

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getIconUrl
documentation:
Returns the URL to p_self's "add" or "search" icon (depending on
p_add), to be used as a background image or not (depending on
p_asBG).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getColSets
documentation:
Gets the ColSet instances corresponding to every showable set of
columns.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getCurrentColumns
documentation:
Gets the columns defined for the current colset, whose p_identifier
is given. The list of available p_colsets is also given.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.isNumbered
documentation: Must we show the order number of every tied object ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getMenuUrl
documentation:
We must provide the URL of the p_tied object, when shown in a Ref
field in render mode 'menus'. If self.menuUrlMethod is specified,
use it. Else, returns the "normal" URL of the view page for the tied
object, but without any navigation information, because in this
render mode, tied object's order is lost and navigation is
impossible.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getMenuCss
documentation: Gets the CSS class that will be applied to this p_zone

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getBatch
documentation:
This method returns a Batch instance in the single objective of
collecting batch-related information that might be present in the
request.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getBatchFor
documentation: Return a Batch instance for a p_total number of objects

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.hasSortIndex
documentation:
An indexed Ref field is not sortable. So an additional index is
required.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.linkObject
documentation:
Links object p_p (which can be a list of objects) to object p_o
through this Ref field.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.unlinkObject
documentation:
Unlinks p_p (which can be a list of objects) from p_o through this
Ref field and return the number of objects truly unlinked.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getStorableValue
documentation:
Convert this p_value, being a object ID or a list of object IDs, to a
list of objects.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.store
py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.mayLink
documentation:
If p_self defines a link method, this method evaluates it and returns
its result. Else, the method returns True.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.mayAdd
documentation:
May the user create (if p_mode == "create") or link
(if mode == "link") (a) new tied object(s) from p_o via this Ref ?
If p_checkMayEdit is False, it means that the condition of being
allowed to edit this Ref field has already been checked somewhere
else (it is always required, we just want to avoid checking it
twice).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.checkAdd
documentation:
Compute m_mayAdd above, and raise an Unauthorized exception if
m_mayAdd returns False.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getOnAdd
documentation: Computes the JS code to execute when button "add" is clicked

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getOnUnlink
documentation: Computes the JS code to execute when button "unlink" is clicked

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getAddLabel
documentation:
Gets the label of the button allowing to add a new tied object. If
p_inMenu, the label must contain the name of the class whose instance
will be created by clincking on the button.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getListLabel
documentation:
If self.link == "list", a label must be shown in front of the list.
Moreover, the label is different if the list is a pick list or the
list of tied objects.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.mayUnlinkElement
documentation: May we unlink p_p from p_o via this Ref field ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getCbJsInit
documentation:
When checkboxes are enabled, this method defines a JS associative
array (named "_appy_objs_cbs") that will store checkboxes' statuses.
This array is needed because all linked objects are not visible at
the same time (pagination).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getAjaxData
documentation:
Initializes an AjaxData object on the DOM node corresponding to this
Ref field.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getHookFor
documentation:
When p_o is shown as a tied object from p_self, this method computes
the ID of the ajax-refreshable hook tag containing p_o (row in a
list, div in a grid...).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getAjaxDataRow
documentation:
Initializes an AjaxData object on the DOM node corresponding to
p_parentHook = a row within the list of referred objects.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.moveObject
documentation: Moves a tied object up/down/top/bottom

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.doCreateWithoutForm
documentation:
This method is called when a user wants to create a object from a
reference field, automatically (without displaying a form).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getReferenceLabel
documentation:
Only works for a Ref with link=True. Displays, on an edit view, the
p_tied object in the select field allowing the user to choose which
object(s) to link through the Ref. The information to display may
only be the object title or more if "shownInfo" is used.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getIndexOf
documentation: Gets the position of p_tied object within this field on p_o

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getPageIndexOf
documentation: Returns the index of the first object of the page where p_tied is

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.sort
documentation: Called by the UI to sort the content of this field

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.gotoTied
documentation:
Redirect to the object tied to p_o via p_self, whose position is
given by request key "number".

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getRenderMode
documentation:
Gets the render mode, determined by self.render and some
exceptions.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getTitleMode
documentation: How will we render the tied objects' titles ?

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getPopupLink
documentation:
Gets the link leading to the page to show in the popup for selecting
objects.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getSelector
documentation:
When this Ref field is shown in a popup for selecting objects to be
included in an "originator" Ref field, this method gets info from the
request about this originator.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getRequestObjects
documentation: Get a list of objects from p_requestValue

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getPopupObjects
documentation:
Gets the list of objects that were selected in the popup (for fields
with link="popup" or "popupRef").

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.onSelectFromPopup
documentation:
This method is called on Ref fields with link=popup[Ref], when
a user has selected objects from the popup, to be added to existing
tied objects, from the view widget.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getLinkBackUrl
documentation:
Get the URL to redirect the user to after having (un)linked an object
via this Ref.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.onLink
documentation: Links or unlinks an object whose id is in the request

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.renderMinimal
documentation:
Render tied p_objects in render mode "minimal" (or "links" if p_links
is True).

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getNavInfo
documentation:
Gets the navigation info allowing to navigate from tied object number
p_nb to its siblings.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getCollapseInfo
documentation:
Returns a Collapsible instance, that determines if the "tied objects"
or "available objects" zone (depending on p_inPickList) is collapsed
or expanded.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getSupTitle
documentation:
Returns the custom chunk of info that must be rendered just before
the p_tied object's title.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getSubTitle
documentation:
Returns the custom chunk of info that must be rendered just after
the p_tied object's title.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.dumpSeparator
documentation:
r_eturns a Separator instance if one must be inserted between
p_previous and p_next objects.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getActions
documentation: Return the custom actions that must be shown among global actions

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getField
documentation:
Returns the sub-field whose name in p_subName, among
p_self.actions.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.getAssocLine
documentation:
Returns the chunk of code allowing to render this Ref as an
association within a class diagram.

documentation: get objects to possibly select them as tied objects for

class_variables.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.PV_SEARCH
documentation: get objects to select them as values for searching base

class_variables.name: py3.appy.model.fields.ref.Ref.PV_FILTER

classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.getDiffValue
documentation:
If p_i is None, the method returns the content of this field on p_o,
rendered with the cumulative diffs between successive versions, from
the very first stored version in p_o's history to the currently
stored value.

If p_i is not None, it is the index of a Change event in p_o's
history, and the method returns a diff between the value before this
change was applied, and its subsequent version, either to be found
in a more recent change in p_o's history or, failing that, the
currently stored value on p_o.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.getUniFormattedValue
documentation:
Returns the formatted variant of p_value. If p_contentLanguage is
specified, p_value is the p_contentLanguage part of a multilingual
value.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.getItalicized
documentation: Returns the set of words to italicize, if relevant

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.getXhtmlCleaner
documentation: Returns a Cleaner instance tailored to p_self

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.validateUniValue
documentation: Ensure p_value as will be stored (=cleaned) is valid XHTML

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.getSearchValue
documentation:
The input widget for a Rich field is of type "text". So behave like a
Text field in that case.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.getHistoryValue
documentation:
For a Rich field, instead of showing the previous p_value in p_o's
history, show a XHTML diff between p_value and a newer version of it
in p_o's history before p_i, or, if not found, the value as currently
stored on p_o.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.getUniStorableValue
documentation: Gets the p_value as can be stored in the database within p_o

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.injectContent
documentation:
When p_self.inject is True, this method replaces every "injection
link" found in p_value with the appropriate content.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.getTextareaStyle
documentation: On "edit", get the content of textarea's "style" attribute

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.getTextareaCols
documentation:
When this widget must be rendered as an HTML field of type
"textarea", get the content of its "cols" attribute.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.getCkLanguage
documentation:
Gets the language for CK editor SCAYT. p_language is one of
self.languages if the field is multilingual, None else. If p_language
is not supported by CK, we use english.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.getCkParams
documentation: Gets the base params to set on a rich text field

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.getJs
documentation: A rich field depend on CKeditor

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.getJsInit
documentation:
Gets the Javascript init code for displaying a rich editor for this
field (rich field only). If the field is multilingual, we must init
the rich text editor for a given p_language (among self.languages).
Else, p_languages is None.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.getJsInlineInit
documentation:
Gets the Javascript init code for enabling inline edition of this
field (rich text only). If the field is multilingual, the current
p_language is given and p_name includes it. Else, p_language is
None.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.isInnerable
documentation: Rich fields cannot be used as inner fields

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.upload
documentation: Uploads a document to the Ref mentioned in attribute "documents"

inner_classes.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.Layouts

methods.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.Layouts.getDefault
documentation: Default layouts for this Rich p_field

class_variables.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.Layouts.c
documentation: Idem but with history

class_variables.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.Layouts.f
documentation: Field only (no label) on view

class_variables.name: py3.appy.model.fields.rich.Rich.Layouts.gc
documentation: Idem but with history
Rich-specific layouts

Field allowing to encode a "rich" text, based on XHTML and external
editor "ckeditor".

Returns the Javascript code to execute for linking the toolbar to a
given poor widget (several widgets may be there if p_self is an
inner field).

When used as an inner field, the toolbar must be rendered only once,
within the container field's header row corresponding to this
field.

Show the toolbar if the field is not inner. Indeed, in that latter

The unique Model instance is created by the
appy.model.loader.Loader.

documentation: Returns the list of root classes for this app

methods.name: py3.appy.tr.po.File.update
documentation:
Updates the existing messages with p_newMessages.
If p_removeNotNew is True, all messages in self.messages
that are not in p_newMessages will be removed. If p_keepOrder is
False, self.messages will be sorted according to p_newMessages. Else,
p_newMessages that are not yet in self.messages will be appended to
the end of self.messages.

classes.name: py3.appy.xml.unmarshaller.Unmarshaller

methods.name: py3.appy.xml.unmarshaller.Unmarshaller.__init__
documentation: This parser assumes that the XML file respects this convention: any tag
may define in attribute "type" storing the type of its content, which may
be:
bool * int * float * long * bytes
datetime * DateTime * tuple * list * object
If "object" is specified, it means that the tag contains sub-tags, each
one corresponding to the value of an attribute for this object. if "tuple"
is specified, it will be converted to a list. "long" is supported for
backward compatibility with Python 2.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.base.Base.computeCid
documentation: Computes the container ID ("cid") for p_self
Containment
Attribute "cid" stores identifying information about the object being the
container of this one. "cid" stands for "*c*ontainer *id*". An object
being tied to another one via a Ref field with attribute "composite" being
True defines such a "container relationship" between these objects, one
object being the container of the other. In that case, the contained
object will have a "cid" storing info about the container object and the
Ref field. More precisely, if:
object X has a composite Ref R storing object Y, with back Ref B,
the container ID for Y (Y.cid) will be equal to "<X.iid>_<B.name>"
An object may be top-level and have no container. Such an object is named
a "root object" and its "cid" will be None.
It is illegal to have the same object being the target of more that one
composite Ref.

methods.name: py3.appy.model.base.Base.getContainer
documentation:
Returns p_self's container, as a tuple (containerObject, fieldName)
or the container alone if p_objectOnly is True. If the object is
root, it returns (None, None) or None. If the container is not found
and p_orInitiator is True, the initiator (which is probably the
future container), when present, is returned.

methods.name: py3.appy.xml.marshaller.Marshaller.marshallSpecificElements
documentation:
You can use this marshaller as a base class for creating your own.
In this case, this method will be called by m_marshall to allow your
concrete marshaller to insert more things in the result. p_r is the
StringIO buffer where the result of the marshalling process is
currently dumped; p_o is the object being currently marshalled.

Allows to produce the XML version of a Python object. The XML result
respects conventions as described in appy.xml.unmarshaller.py.

modules.name: py3.appy.utils.string
documentation: Utility module related to string manipulation

functions.name: py3.appy.utils.string.getStringFrom
documentation:
Returns a string representation for p_o that can be transported over
HTTP and manipulated in Javascript.

If p_stringify is True, non-string literals (None, integers, floats...)
are surrounded by this p_c(har). String literals are always surrounded by
p_c(hars).

functions.name: py3.appy.utils.string.getDictFrom
documentation:
Returns a dict from string representation p_s of the form
"key1:value1,key2:value2".

functions.name: py3.appy.utils.string.sadd
documentation:
Appends sub-string p_sub into p_s, which is a list of sub-strings
separated by p_sep, and returns the updated string. If p_append is False,
p_sub is inserted at the start of p_s instead.

functions.name: py3.appy.utils.string.sremove
documentation:
Removes sub-string p_sub from p_s, which is a list of sub-strings
separated by p_sep, and returns the updated string.

functions.name: py3.appy.utils.string.stringIsAmong
documentation:
Is p_s among list of strings p_l ? p_s can be a string or a
list/tuple of strings. In this latter case, r_ is True if at least
one string among p_s is among p_l.

functions.name: py3.appy.utils.string.stretchText
documentation:
Inserts occurrences of p_char within p_s according to p_pattern.
Example: stretchText("475123456", (3,2,2,2)) returns "475 12 34 56".

functions.name: py3.appy.utils.string.grammarJoin
documentation:
Joins list p_l with p_sep, excepted the last 2 elements that are joined
with p_lastSep. grammarJoin(["a", "b", "c"]) produces "a, b and c".

classes.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.Merger

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.Merger.computeNewDiffs
documentation:
lineB may include inner "insert" and/or tags. This function
detects them.

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.Merger.getNextDiff
documentation:
During the merging process on self.lineB, what next diff to
"consume"? An old one? A new one?

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.Merger.manageBackOverlap
documentation:
p_newDiff has been removed from self.lineB. Here we check if there
is no overlap with inserts from self.lineA, ie, text that was
inserted in one if the many cumulative updates from self.lineA and
that was deleted in self.lineB.

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.Merger.manageOverlap
documentation:
p_oldDiff is a previously inserted text from self.lineA. This text
is not found anymore at the start of self.lineB[self.i:]: it means
that an overlapping diff exists among new diffs. We will manage this
by identifying several, cutted, "insert" and/or "edit" zones.

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.Merger.dumpNewDiff
documentation: Computes p_newDiff as it must appear in the result and return it.

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.Merger.dumpDiff
documentation:
Computes the next p_diff (starting at p_diffStart) to insert into the
result and return it. If p_isPrevious is True, the diff is an old one
(from self.lineA); else, it is a new one (from self.lineB).

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.Merger.merge
documentation: Merges self.previousDiffs into self.lineB.

inner_classes.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.Merger.MergeError
documentation: Exception that may be raised by this class if the merge fails.

This class allows to merge 2 lines of text, each containing inserts and
deletions.

classes.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.HtmlDiff

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.HtmlDiff.getModifiedChunk
documentation:
p_sep.join(p_seq) (if p_seq is a list) or p_seq (if p_seq is a
string) is a chunk that was either inserted (p_type='insert') or
deleted (p_type='delete'). This method will surround this part with
a div or span tag that will get some CSS class allowing to highlight
the update.

If p_msg is given, it will be used instead of the default
p_type-related message stored on p_self.

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.HtmlDiff.applyDiff
documentation:
p_diff is a regex containing an insert or delete that was found
within line. This function applies the diff, removing or inserting
the diff into p_line.

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.HtmlDiff.isSimilar
documentation:
Returns True if strings p_s1 and p_s2 can be considered as
similar.

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.HtmlDiff.getLineAndType
py3.appy.utils.diff.HtmlDiff.computeTag
documentation:
p_regexTag is a re.MatchObject from regex htmlTag. p_content is a
new content to put within this tag. This method produces the new
string tag filled with p_content.

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.HtmlDiff.getSeqDiff
documentation:
p_seqA and p_seqB are lists of strings. Here we will try to identify
similarities between strings from p_seqA and p_seqB, and return a
list of differences between p_seqA and p_seqB, where each element
is a tuple (action, line).
* If p_action is "delete", "line" is a line of p_seqA considered as
  not included anymore in p_seqB;
* If p_action is "insert", "line" is a line of p_seqB considered as
  not included in p_seqA;
* If p_action is "replace", "line" is a tuple
  (lineA, lineB, previousDiffsA) containing one line from p_seqA and
  one from p_seqB considered as similar. "previousDiffsA" contains
  potential previous inner diffs that were found (but extracted
  from, for comparison purposes) lineA.

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.HtmlDiff.split
documentation:
Splits string p_s with p_sep. If p_sep is a space, the split can't
happen for a leading or trailing space, which must be considered as
being part of the first or last word.

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.HtmlDiff.removeGarbage
documentation:
Removes from list p_l elements that have no interest, like blank
strings or considered as is. Also: strip lines (ie, if sep is a
carriage return.

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.HtmlDiff.getStringDiff
py3.appy.utils.diff.HtmlDiff.getDumpPrefix
documentation:
In most cases, when concatenating the next diff (p_add) to the
global result (p_res), I must prefix it with p_sep (excepted if p_res
is still empty). But when p_sep is a space, no space must be inserted
between 2 adjacent updates (p_add and p_previousAdd), because such a
space was not in the original version. This method computes the
prefix, that can thus be empty if this latter case is met.

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.HtmlDiff.getReplacement
documentation:
p_chunkA has been replaced with p_chunkB. Compute this update and
return it.

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.HtmlDiff.getHtmlDiff
documentation:
Returns the differences between p_old and p_new. Result is a string
containing the comparison in HTML format. p_sep is used for turning
p_old and p_new into sequences. If p_sep is a carriage return, this
method is used for performing a whole diff between 2 strings splitted
into sequences of lines; if sep is a space, the diff is a
word-by-word comparison within 2 lines that have been detected as
similar in a previous call to m_getHtmlDiff with sep=carriage
return.

methods.name: py3.appy.utils.diff.HtmlDiff.get
documentation: Produces the result

This class allows to compute differences between two versions of some
HTML chunk.

Produce a XHTML version of the output of a the Unix diff (or derivates,

functions.name: py3.appy.utils.addListValue

functions.name: py3.appy.utils.path.getTempFileName

p_path is a file path that can contain occurrences of "." and "..". This
